title: heuchera pilosissima var. typica
intro: Heuchera pilosissima var. typica, also known as the hairy alum root, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family. The hairy alum root has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The hairy alum root can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
